refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly specify the latency percentile and network conditions to clarify acceptance criteria and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency over the network link under agreed conditions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms during teleoperation sessions.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission over the network link to avoid latency spikes.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with bounded delay.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized low-latency communication protocols and data compression.
DP2: Implement edge computing at vehicle and operator ends to reduce processing delays.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation traffic on the network link with QoS mechanisms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use optimized low-latency communication protocols and data compression.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between communication protocol and network link performance
Dependency on vehicle and teleoperation software synchronization
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates latency reduction to communication protocol optimization, minimizing cross-domain coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of latency spikes due to unpredictable network congestion.
Assumes vehicle and teleoperation modules can support protocol changes.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge computing (DP2) if latency targets are not met by protocol optimization alone.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and hardware components responsible for remote control, communication protocol implementation, and latency management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection carrying teleoperation data with latency constraints.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time vehicle status data received for operator feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands sent from operator to vehicle control systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of latency-critical functions; low diffusion as protocol logic is centralized.
Some latency-sensitive processing occurs here but less control over communication protocol.
Broad scope but less focused containment of latency-specific logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing latency-critical communication protocol logic within the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on vehicle hardware capabilities may require cross-module coordination.
Network link variability may impact latency despite module containment.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor cross-module dependencies and consider co-locating some edge processing in Vehicle if latency targets are not met.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (optimized communication protocols) directly addresses the refined FR sub-requirements with clear verification paths.

diffusion_risks: Some latency-related processing in Vehicle module may cause diffusion if not well coordinated.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment of DP logic, minimizing diffusion risks.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end across the Teleoperation module interfaces using network monitoring tools.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency-critical functions to Teleoperation module with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Track cross-module dependencies between Teleoperation and Vehicle for latency-sensitive processing.
Validate network link performance assumptions during integration testing.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, risks related to network variability and cross-module dependencies require refinement and possible inclusion of edge computing DP.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission over the network link to avoid latency spikes.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with bounded delay.

revise.why: To incorporate additional latency reduction strategies such as edge computing and QoS prioritization to mitigate identified risks.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can support protocol changes.
open_risks: Latency spikes due to network congestion.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under real network conditions.
